answer: {"name": "search_clinical_guidelines", "parameters": {"query":"SGLT2 inhibitors for type 2 diabetes with chronic kidney disease eGFR >=20 mL/min/1.73m2",top_k":"5"}}

arguments.query: SGLT2 inhibitors in type 2 diabetes with chronic kidney disease

result.query: SGLT2 inhibitors in type 2 diabetes with chronic kidney disease

results.content: In patients with type 2 diabetes and chronic kidney disease, an SGLT2 inhibitor is recommended for those with eGFR >=20 mL/min/1.73m2 to reduce CKD progression and cardiovascular risk. ACE inhibitors or ARBs are first-line for patients with albuminuria and hypertension.
results.source: ADA Standards of Care in Diabetes
results.citation: Chronic Kidney Disease in Diabetes, American Diabetes Association, 2024

results.content: In patients with heart failure with reduced ejection fraction (HFrEF), guideline-directed medical therapy includes ARNI/ACEi/ARB, beta-blockers, mineralocorticoid receptor antagonists, and SGLT2 inhibitors. SGLT2 inhibitors are recommended regardless of diabetes status.
An A1C goal for many nonpregnant adults with diabetes of <7% without significant hypoglycemia is appropriate. Metformin is the preferred initial pharmacologic agent for type 2 diabetes.
results.source: ADA Standards of Care in Diabetes
results.citation: Glycemic Targets & Pharmacologic Approaches, American Diabetes Association, 2024

results.content: CKD staging is based on eGFR and albuminuria categories. SGLT2 inhibitors and ACE inhibitors or ARBs are recommended to slow progression in patients with albuminuric CKD, regardless of diabetes status.
results.source: KDIGO Clinical Practice Guideline for CKD
results.citation: Evaluation and Management of CKD, KDIGO, 2024

results.content: GLP-1 receptor agonists and dual GIP/GLP-1 agonists are recommended for weight management in patients with type 2 diabetes and obesity, in addition to lifestyle intervention, given their combined glycemic and weight benefits.
results.source: ADA Standards of Care in Diabetes
results.citation: Obesity Management in Type 2 Diabetes, American Diabetes Association, 2024

results.content: A blood pressure target of <130/80 mmHg is recommended for most adults with diabetes and hypertension. ACE inhibitors or ARBs are preferred first-line agents, particularly in the presence of albuminuria.
results.source: ADA Standards of Care in Diabetes
results.citation: Hypertension Management in Diabetes, American Diabetes Association, 2024

results.content: High-intensity statin therapy is recommended for patients with clinical atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease and for primary prevention in adults 40-75 years with LDL-C >=190 mg/dL or diabetes with additional risk factors.

The standard regimen for drug-susceptible pulmonary tuberculosis is a 6-month course of isoniazid, rifampicin, pyrazinamide, and ethambutol for 2 months, followed by isoniazid and rifampicin for 4 months.
A blood pressure target of <130/80 mmHg is recommended for most adults with hypertension. First-line agents include thiazide diuretics, ACE inhibitors, ARBs, and calcium channel blockers.

Oral anticoagulation is recommended for patients with atrial fibrillation and a CHA2DS2-VASc score of 2 or more in men or 3 or more in women. Direct oral anticoagulants are preferred over warfarin in most eligible patients.

result.disclaimer: Evidence excerpts for professional review; not a diagnosis.
